explanation: The Sigma rule detects uncommon child processes of userinit.exe. 'cmd.exe' is a common child process and would not trigger the rule. This is a safe test command to simulate a benign process.
PowerShell is a known legitimate child process of userinit.exe, especially on Windows Core systems. This command simulates a benign PowerShell invocation that would be filtered by the rule.
Using the full path to cmd.exe simulates a process creation event. Since cmd.exe is a known legitimate child process of userinit.exe, it would not trigger the Sigma rule.
This is an alternative path to PowerShell with a simplified command line. It is a legitimate child process of userinit.exe and would be filtered by the rule.
Explorer.exe is explicitly filtered in the Sigma rule. This command simulates a benign process that would not trigger the rule.
